title: Admin trash — soft-deleted sessions, restorable
problem: Sessions soft-delete via DELETE /api/sessions/:id; the row stays in D1 with deleted_at set. AdminTrash is the recovery surface — lists soft-deleted sessions, shows when each was deleted and by whom, and offers a one-click restore. Without this, recovering an accidentally-deleted session would require SQL access.
analysis: GET /api/sessions?deleted=true (admin-only opt-in flag) on mount; returns ONLY rows with deleted_at IS NOT NULL. Each row shows: visitor email, problem type, original status (last value before delete), when deleted, who deleted (self vs admin). Restore button → PATCH /api/sessions/:id with { restore: true } which clears deleted_at and resets status to 'triage' (the safest restoration target — admin decides next move from there).

decisions.decision: Restore lands at status='triage', not the pre-delete status
decisions.rationale: Restoring directly to whatever status the session held before delete could put it back into active capacity (breaking the cap rule). Landing at 'triage' is safe — admin reviews and transitions explicitly. Worst case: a restored session needs one PATCH to active. Best case: the deletion was correct and the restoration is a clean re-triage.
decisions.recommendation: Keep the triage-restore. If an admin wants to restore to exact prior state, they can do that via two PATCHes (restore + transition).

successCriteria:
- Lists only soft-deleted sessions
- Restore button works in one click
- Restored session lands at status='triage'
- Visitors never see soft-deleted sessions on /me regardless of admin actions
- Forbidden message for non-admin visitors who somehow reach the page
implementation: src/pages/AdminTrash.tsx — useEffect → listSessions(?deleted=true). undeleteSession() (src/lib/sessionsApi.ts) wraps the restore PATCH. Confirmation modal omitted; restore is reversible (just delete again).
userGuide: Visit /admin/trash. Click restore on a row to bring it back to triage. To permanently delete: there's no UI; SQL only (DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = ?). Soft-delete is the visitor-facing 'delete' semantic; hard-delete only happens via /api/me's full erasure flow.
knownLimitations: No bulk restore — one click per row
No filter / search; current scale doesn't warrant
Restored sessions go to triage even if originally far past that — admin re-evaluates

statusHistory.reason: Page shipped with the trash list + one-click restore.
Code-first doc co-located. Restore-to-triage decision stable.
